blockingIssues.description: fetchLiveNews() still falls back to GDELT_QUERY_PROFILES when no explicit queries are supplied, even though getDefaultQueryProfiles() was introduced for the new region/language coverage. Both refreshSnapshot() and the default client/server fallbacks call fetchLiveNews() without a queries argument, so the new sourcecountry:/sourcelang: profiles are dead code in the main ingest path.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Use getDefaultQueryProfiles() for the no-query default path (or otherwise merge GDELT_REGION_QUERIES and GDELT_LANGUAGE_QUERIES into the normal ingest query set), then add a regression test that asserts the default profile count includes the advanced query groups.

blockingIssues.description: Four of the eight newly added HTML candidates duplicate outlets that already exist as RSS feeds: The Irrawaddy (src/services/rssService.js:223), Khmer Times (src/services/rssService.js:231), Asia-Plus (src/services/rssService.js:238), and Haiti Libre (src/services/rssService.js:302). Because those publishers already have working RSS sources, this change contributes only four genuinely new non-RSS outlets and falls short of the feature goal to add 5+ HTML sources for sites that do not offer RSS.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Replace the overlapping candidates with outlets that truly lack RSS feeds, or explicitly disable the redundant HTML entries and add additional non-RSS sources so the feature contributes at least five genuinely new HTML-only sources.

appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/source-catalog.md documenting that source-catalog ISO normalization uses exact countryToIso() matches, so alias-style country names need canonical labels or explicit ISO codes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that strict TDD can be relaxed for purely declarative data-catalog expansion tasks, where the useful verification work is often adding or updating assertions after the data change rather than forcing an artificial red/green cycle.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The expand-rss-feeds-africa-mena handoff explicitly called out that the backend-worker TDD procedure did not fit a feed-catalog expansion and only required a coverage-audit assertion update after Oman gained local feeds.
